evals.prompt: Tune a slow D1 query without changing schema or migration files.
evals.expected_behavior: The response treats the task as runtime D1 query tuning, inspects the exact D1 binding and environment first, and focuses on query or adapter rewrites instead of schema changes.
assertions: Mentions inspecting the exact D1 binding or Wrangler config before tuning.
Mentions reproducing the slow path or using EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN / wrangler d1 execute.
Avoids recommending schema, index, or migration changes as part of the tuning workflow.

evals.prompt: This Worker does N+1 D1 lookups in a loop. I need a better query path without changing migrations.
evals.expected_behavior: The response identifies the access-pattern problem and recommends a set-based rewrite that stays within the no-schema constraint.
assertions: Mentions N+1 or repeated round trips as the performance issue.
Suggests a set-based query, join, batch, or query consolidation rather than row-by-row follow-up lookups.
Keeps the focus on the D1 query or adapter layer.

evals.prompt: This D1 query is slow and EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN still shows a wide scan. The only real fix looks like a new index, but I was asked not to change schema.
evals.expected_behavior: The response respects the no-schema constraint, says the real fix is out of scope, and redirects to the migration workflow instead of pretending a query-only change solved it.
assertions: States plainly that the no-schema constraint blocks the real fix.
Redirects index or schema work to cloudflare-d1-migrations.
Does not present a schema change as part of the current tuning workflow.
